items.summary: STORY: Chinese startup DeepSeek is developing its own AI chip, according to three people familiar with the matter, a push that could reduce its reliance on Nvidia and Huawei chips, which it has depended on to train and run its globally popular models.If successful, DeepSeek's expansion into semiconductor development would mark a major strategic shift for a company widely hailed in China as the country's AI champion.Shares of U.S.-based Nvidia slipped in morning trading before paring those losses.Bennett said it's "basic economics" that a company like Nvidia with "outsized" returns would attract increased competition. She also noted that China has "repeatedly said they want to reduce dependency on the U.S. as it relates to AI, and so they are significantly spending money, investing money, around a lot of the Chinese-based companies."
